---
title: "Auto Loan Credit — May 2018"
subtitle: "ABS-EE Loan-Level Panel · SEC EDGAR Securitization Filings"
description: "706,856 loans across 12 auto ABS trusts, reporting April 2018 performance. Pre-computed from raw SEC EDGAR ABS-EE filings via DuckDB."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<div class="data-note">
  <strong>Reporting period:</strong> April 30, 2018 —
  20 ABS-EE parquet files · 12 trusts · 706,856 loans · Aggregated from SEC EDGAR Form ABS-EE.
  See <a href="../data-sources.qmd">Data Sources</a> for full methodology.
</div>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

TRUSTS = [
  {name:"Ally Auto 2017-1",               loans:118931, avg_bal:22061, avg_fico:729},
  {name:"Hyundai Auto 2017-A",            loans: 71274, avg_bal:21192, avg_fico:739},
  {name:"Toyota Auto Recv. 2017-A",       loans: 69923, avg_bal:26015, avg_fico:760},
  {name:"Ally Auto 2017-2",               loans: 67717, avg_bal:21490, avg_fico:726},
  {name:"Honda Auto Recv. 2017-1",        loans: 63515, avg_bal:23783, avg_fico:763},
  {name:"CarMax Auto 2017-1",             loans: 61139, avg_bal:19003, avg_fico:703},
  {name:"Santander Drive 2017-1",         loans: 52577, avg_bal:20177, avg_fico:602},
  {name:"Ford Credit Auto 2017-A",        loans: 50945, avg_bal:32292, avg_fico:645},
  {name:"World Omni Auto 2017-A",         loans: 50000, avg_bal:27059, avg_fico:720},
  {name:"Nissan Auto Recv. 2017-A",       loans: 41719, avg_bal:27849, avg_fico:776},
  {name:"AmeriCredit Auto 2017-1",        loans: 38379, avg_bal:23173, avg_fico:574},
  {name:"California Republic 2017-1",     loans: 20737, avg_bal:20708, avg_fico:700}
]

FICO = [
  {bucket:"Sub-600", n:81610,  ord:1},
  {bucket:"600–649", n:61018,  ord:2},
  {bucket:"650–699", n:128212, ord:3},
  {bucket:"700–749", n:145972, ord:4},
  {bucket:"750–799", n:124419, ord:5},
  {bucket:"800+",    n:154419, ord:6}
]

STATES = [
  {state:"CA", n:93719}, {state:"TX", n:80416}, {state:"FL", n:74315},
  {state:"IL", n:33066}, {state:"GA", n:31012}, {state:"NC", n:29076},
  {state:"PA", n:26076}, {state:"NY", n:22126}, {state:"OH", n:18968},
  {state:"NJ", n:18900}
]

RATES = [
  {bucket:"< 2%",  n:106656, ord:1},
  {bucket:"2–4%",  n:156536, ord:2},
  {bucket:"4–6%",  n:117585, ord:3},
  {bucket:"6–8%",  n: 74020, ord:4},
  {bucket:"8–10%", n: 43441, ord:5},
  {bucket:"10%+",  n:114504, ord:6}
]

VINTAGE = [
  {year:"2011", n:3692},  {year:"2012", n:35285},
  {year:"2013", n:29860}, {year:"2014", n:50597},
  {year:"2015", n:134061},{year:"2016", n:421175},
  {year:"2017", n:32103}
]

DELINQ = [
  {bucket:"Current",    n:470136, ord:1},
  {bucket:"1 period",   n:2285,   ord:2},
  {bucket:"2 periods",  n:2825,   ord:3},
  {bucket:"3 periods",  n:2810,   ord:4},
  {bucket:"4+ periods", n:51764,  ord:5}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

bandForFico = (score) => score >= 740
  ? "Prime (740+)"
  : score >= 700
    ? "Near-prime (700-739)"
    : score >= 650
      ? "Mid (650-699)"
      : "Subprime (<650)"

shortTrustName = (name) => name
  .replace(" Auto Receivables ", " ")
  .replace(" Automobile ", " ")
  .replace(" Receivables ", " ")
  .replace(" Drive Auto ", " Drive ")
  .replace(" Credit Auto ", " Credit ")
  .replace(" California Republic ", " Calif. Republic ")
  .replace(" 2017-", " '17-")

TRUSTS_VIEW = TRUSTS
  .map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.loans * 100) / 706856, fico_band: bandForFico(d.avg_fico) }))
  .sort((a, b) => b.loans - a.loans)
  .slice(0, 10)
  .map((d) => ({ ...d, short_name: shortTrustName(d.name) }))

STATES_VIEW = STATES
  .map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.n * 100) / 529820 }))
  .sort((a, b) => b.n - a.n)
  .slice(0, 10)

FICO_VIEW = FICO.map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.n * 100) / 695650 }))
RATES_VIEW = RATES.map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.n * 100) / 612742 }))
VINTAGE_VIEW = VINTAGE.map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.n * 100) / 706856 }))
DELINQ_VIEW = DELINQ.map((d) => ({ ...d, share: (d.n * 100) / 529820 }))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// KPI strip
html`
<div class="showcase-meta">
  <span class="showcase-period">April 2018 reporting period</span>
  <span class="static-badge">curated snapshot</span>
</div>
<div class="kpi-strip">
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">706,856</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Total Loans</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">12</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">ABS Trusts</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">712</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Avg FICO</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">$21,945</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Median Balance</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">5.52%</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Avg Rate</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-value">63.5%</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">New Vehicles</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

---

## Portfolio Composition {#portfolio-composition .showcase-section}

::: {.grid .showcase-grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

**Trust loan counts** — 12 trusts, all 2017 vintage, reporting April 2018 performance.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const xMax = Math.max(...TRUSTS_VIEW.map((d) => d.loans)) * 1.3
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 160,
    marginRight: 80,
    height: 320,
    x: { axis: null, domain: [0, xMax] },
    y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(TRUSTS_VIEW, {
        x: "loans",
        y: "short_name",
        fill: d => d.avg_fico >= 740 ? "#4A6FA5"
                 : d.avg_fico >= 700 ? "#7BA3C8"
                 : d.avg_fico >= 650 ? "#B8C8DC"
                 : "#C86A50",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(TRUSTS_VIEW, {
        x: "loans",
        y: "short_name",
        text: d => `${(d.loans / 1000).toFixed(0)}K`,
        dx: 8,
        textAnchor: "start",
        fill: "#6E6E6E",
        fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">Color = avg FICO band: blue ≥ 740 · mid-blue ≥ 700 · light ≥ 650 · red < 650</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

**Top 10 states by loan count** — CA, TX, FL account for 35% of the pool.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const xMax = Math.max(...STATES_VIEW.map((d) => d.n)) * 1.3
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 36,
    marginRight: 75,
    height: 320,
    x: { axis: null, domain: [0, xMax] },
    y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(STATES_VIEW, {
        x: "n",
        y: "state",
        fill: "#C8A058",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(STATES_VIEW, {
        x: "n",
        y: "state",
        text: d => `${(d.n/1000).toFixed(0)}K`,
        dx: 8,
        textAnchor: "start",
        fill: "#6E6E6E",
        fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">obligorGeographicLocation field · 529K loans with state reported</p>
:::
:::

---

## Loan Characteristics {#loan-characteristics .showcase-section}

::: {.grid .showcase-grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**FICO distribution** — avg 712, skewed prime.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const ficoColors = ["#C86A50","#C8964A","#C8B86A","#7EA870","#5A8A8A","#4A6FA5"]
  const total = FICO_VIEW.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 300,
    marginTop: 20,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(FICO_VIEW, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        fill: (d, i) => ficoColors[i],
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(FICO_VIEW, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n * 100 / total).toFixed(1) + "%",
        dy: -10,
        fontSize: 11,
        fill: "#6E6E6E"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8", strokeWidth: 1 })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">695K scored · Sub-600 = 11.5% · 800+ = 21.8%</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**Origination vintage** — 59% of pool is 2016 origination.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 300,
    marginTop: 20,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(VINTAGE_VIEW, {
        x: "year",
        y: "n",
        fill: d => d.year === "2016" ? "#4A6FA5" : "#D4CFC4",
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(VINTAGE_VIEW.filter(d => d.year === "2016"), {
        x: "year",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n/1000).toFixed(0) + "K",
        dy: -10,
        fontSize: 11,
        fill: "#6E6E6E"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8", strokeWidth: 1 })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">Year of loan origination · 2016 vintage = 421K loans (59%)</p>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

**Rate distribution** — avg 5.52%, bimodal (captive vs. bank).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const total = RATES_VIEW.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  return Plot.plot({
    height: 300,
    marginTop: 20,
    x: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    y: { axis: null },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(RATES_VIEW, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        fill: d => d.ord <= 2 ? "#7BA3C8" : d.ord <= 4 ? "#8A7A6A" : "#C86A50",
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(RATES_VIEW, {
        x: "bucket",
        y: "n",
        text: d => (d.n * 100 / total).toFixed(1) + "%",
        dy: -10,
        fontSize: 11,
        fill: "#6E6E6E"
      }),
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#D8D3C8", strokeWidth: 1 })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">612K non-zero rate loans · bimodal: low-rate captive + bank paper</p>
:::
:::

---

## Payment Performance {#payment-performance .showcase-section}

30+ day delinquency of 3.1% among loans with status reported. ~177K loans have no status field in this filing.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const reported = DELINQ_VIEW.reduce((s, d) => s + d.n, 0)
  const xMax = Math.max(...DELINQ_VIEW.map((d) => d.n)) * 1.3
  const delinqColors = {
    "Current":    "#4A6FA5",
    "1 period":   "#D8C090",
    "2 periods":  "#C8A058",
    "3 periods":  "#C87840",
    "4+ periods": "#C85030"
  }
  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 110,
    marginRight: 70,
    height: 300,
    x: { axis: null, domain: [0, xMax] },
    y: { label: null, tickSize: 0 },
    style: { fontFamily: "'DM Mono', monospace", fontSize: "12px", background: "transparent" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(DELINQ_VIEW, {
        x: "n",
        y: "bucket",
        fill: d => delinqColors[d.bucket] || "#D8D3C8",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        rx: 2
      }),
      Plot.text(DELINQ_VIEW, {
        x: "n",
        y: "bucket",
        text: d => `${(d.n/1000).toFixed(0)}K  ${(d.n*100/reported).toFixed(1)}%`,
        dx: 8,
        textAnchor: "start",
        fill: "#6E6E6E",
        fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

<p class="chart-caption">
529,820 loans with status: 88.7% current · 11.3% one or more payments behind.
Status codes: 0 = current, 1–3 = payment periods behind, 4+ = severely delinquent.
</p>

---

## Methodology {#methodology}

**What this page shows** — loan-level performance data from 20 ABS-EE parquet files, covering 12 auto loan securitization trusts that reported April 2018 data in their May 2018 SEC EDGAR filings. Aggregations were pre-computed from the raw loan records using DuckDB.

**Data path** — SEC EDGAR ABS-EE (Form 10-D) → data lake pipeline → Hive-partitioned Parquet in Cloudflare R2 → DuckDB aggregation → embedded in this page as a static snapshot.

**Trust coverage** — these are the trusts that filed for the May 2018 reporting period. Coverage skews toward bank-sponsored trusts (Ally, Toyota, Honda) and away from captive-heavy structures.

For full dataset documentation, see [Data Sources →](../data-sources.qmd)
